commit.message:
feat: application layer — contracts, LLM gateway, services (#61)

* feat: application layer — contracts, LLM gateway, services

Application layer (agents/src/application/) — the shared core that CLI,
Python MCP server, and future Web API all wrap.

Components:
- contracts/agents.py — Pydantic request/response models for all 7 agents,
  pipeline, and digest. Modeled around actual agent capabilities.
- llm/types.py — LLMProvider protocol, LLMMessage, LLMResponse
- llm/gateway.py — LLMGateway with synthesize(), create_gateway() factory
- llm/litellm_provider.py — LiteLLM-based multi-provider implementation
- llm/mock.py — MockProvider (testing) + SkipProvider (MCP path)
- services/agent_service.py — maps typed contracts to agent run() calls
- services/pipeline_service.py — wraps orchestrator with task_id support
- services/digest_service.py — wraps research pipeline
- config.py — AppConfig via pydantic-settings (env vars)

Orchestrator fix:
- Added task_id to AgentTask and AgentResult
- Pipeline now keys by task.id (not agent_name), enabling same agent
  to run multiple times with different task IDs

Documentation updates:
- architecture.md — reflects implemented app layer, task_id, phasing table
- agents.md — orchestration, application services, gateway status
- copilot-instructions.md — updated tech stack versions

Dependencies added:
- pydantic >=2.11
- pydantic-settings >=2.9
- litellm >=1.72

Tests: 52 new tests (274 total, 5 skipped)

Closes #49

- Split CI into unit (every PR) and integration (main push only) jobs
- Add vitest.integration.config.ts for TS integration tests
- Update preflight to run unit tests only
- Document test separation conventions in copilot instructions

- Remove test_valid_request and test_response_from_metadata (Pydantic

- Rewrite TestComputeConviction: replace hard-coded penalty values with

- 276 Python tests pass, 45 TypeScript tests pass

commit.message:
Add automatic `flyway` label for PRs touching Flyway migrations (#1280)

### **Behov / Bakgrunn**

PRs som endrer Flyway-migreringer under
`migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/` ble ikke automatisk
merket, noe som gjorde det vanskelig å raskt identifisere
databaseendringer i PR-oversikten.

### **Løsning**

- Legger til `.github/labeler.yml` med glob-regel som trigges på
endringer i `migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**`
- Legger til `.github/workflows/labeler.yml` som kjører
`actions/labeler@v6` på alle PRs og setter `flyway`-labelen automatisk

Forutsetter at `flyway`-labelen er opprettet manuelt i repoet.

### **Andre endringer**

Ingen.

### **Skjermbilder** (hvis relevant)

N/A

changes with the `flyway` label.

> `migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/`
> 
> ## Files to create
> 
> ### `.github/labeler.yml`
> ```yaml
> flyway:
>   - changed-files:
> - any-glob-to-any-file:
'migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**'
> ```

commit.message:
fix: watermark PNG aspect ratio by replacing scale2ref with explicit scale

scale2ref=h=-1 applied the reference video's scale factor to the
watermark height (e.g. height = video_height * fraction), completely
ignoring the watermark's own aspect ratio. A 512×128 logo at 15% of a
1920px video was rendered as 288×162px instead of 288×72px.

Fix: use [1:v]scale={target_px}:-2 where:
- target_px = effective_video_width × imageSize / 100
- -2 preserves the input aspect ratio and rounds to an even number

Changes:
- ffmpeg.rs: add effective_video_width param to build_video_filters;
  replace scale2ref block; update all unit test assertions
- export.rs: read session.width, compute effective_width (clamped to
  max_width if set), pass to extract_frames
- frame.rs: read session.width, pass to extract_frame
- Cargo.toml: add image = "0.25" dev-dependency
- tests/watermark_scale_test.rs: new integration tests (run with
  --ignored) that verify a 512×128 logo is correctly sized at 15% and
  25% of video width using solid-color lavfi test fixtures

All 36 tests pass; 2 ignored integration tests confirmed green.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
